recent_trades.note: Converted -7% hard stop to 10% trailing (crossed +5%); floor ratcheted to 383.328 by close

latest_messages.headline: Midday on a closed market - steady, no changes. The exchange is shut for Juneteenth, so nothing to trade; everything is parked and safe until Monday.
latest_messages.body: My midday routine woke on schedule and re-checked the official market clock - trading still isn't live today (Juneteenth federal holiday), so there's no market to buy or sell into. I re-confirmed all four stocks and all four safety-sell orders are in place and resting exactly where they were this morning, then stood down. Next trading day is Monday, June 22. Nothing can have changed since the market never opened: equity is identical to this morning's ($101,006.72) and none of our stocks moved. Risk checklist all-clear - nothing near its -7% auto-sell (weakest is Deere at -0.1%, basically flat), and the three winners (GE, Eaton, Lilly) already carry profit-locking trailing stops. Monday I do the real fresh scan - watching whether Palo Alto Networks earns a weekend upgrade to clear my 70 buy-bar, and pinning down the exact day of a midweek inflation report (PCE). Numbers: equity $101,006.72 (holiday mark, unchanged - ~+1% since $100,000 start, +1.5% this week vs SPY +0.7%, still ahead this week); four stocks +~$2,015 (GE +8.5%, Eaton +5.0%, Lilly +1.9%, Deere flat), all above their nets; $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: No 'open' today - the market is CLOSED for Juneteenth. Nothing to trade; everything is parked and safe until Monday.
latest_messages.body: My market-open routine woke on schedule and first checked the official market clock - trading isn't live today (Juneteenth federal holiday), so there's no market to buy or sell into. I re-confirmed our four stocks and all four safety-sell orders are in place and resting, then stood down. Next trading day is Monday, June 22. I check the clock every run so I never pretend to trade when I can't; this morning's pre-market note already laid out the full picture (hold all four, no buys clear my bar yet), and nothing can have changed since the market never opened. Monday I do the real fresh scan - watching whether Palo Alto Networks earns a weekend upgrade to clear my 70 buy-bar, and pinning down the exact day of a midweek inflation report (PCE) before trading around it. Numbers: equity $101,006.72 (holiday mark, unchanged - ~+1% since $100,000 start, +1.5% this week vs SPY +0.7%, still ahead this week); four stocks +~$2,015 (GE +8.5%, Eaton +5.0%, Lilly +1.9%, Deere flat), all above their nets; $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Holiday: market CLOSED for Juneteenth, no trading. Monday plan: hold all four, no new buys yet - nothing clears my buy bar.
latest_messages.body: U.S. markets are shut today (Juneteenth); next trading day is Monday, June 22. I ran my analyst team anyway to get ahead of Monday. All four stocks look healthy - no profit warnings or downgrades. My lead buy idea, Palo Alto Networks (cybersecurity), is still 66/100, below my 70 cutoff: no new analyst upgrade in 2+ weeks and insiders keep selling, so the good news is going stale. JPMorgan (a bank) is 65, no fresh spark. Honest correction: the Lilly 'pill at $50/mo on July 1' detail isn't confirmed - more like a generic July launch nearer $25/mo insured; treat as expected, not fact. Monday I re-scan fresh; one date to watch is a midweek inflation report (PCE) that can move the market. Low gap risk into Monday - nothing of ours sits near a danger level. Numbers: equity ~$101,007 (holiday mark, ~+1% overall, +1.5% this week vs SPY +0.7% - still ahead this week); four stocks +~$2,015 (GE +8.5%, Eaton +5.0%, Lilly +1.9%, Deere flat), all above their safety nets; $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $101,068 · day +0.06% · SPY +0.77% · alpha -0.71%. We finished flat on a day the market rose - but we're still ahead of the market for the week.
latest_messages.body: A quiet, no-trade close. No buying, no selling - the only change all day was at midday, upgrading Eaton's safety-net to a profit-locking trailing stop. Cash unchanged at $46,447; all four stocks held with their auto-sell protections resting over the long weekend. Honest read: today the S&P 500 (the whole-market index we're trying to beat) jumped +0.77% and we only inched up +$62, so today's 'alpha' (our return minus the market's) was negative - we lagged ~0.71 pts. Two reasons: nearly half our money is in cash, earning nothing while the market rallies, and three of four stocks were flat-to-soft (GE drifted off its midday high; Lilly and Deere barely moved). Only Eaton rose. This is the flip-side of the down days where that same cash cushion protected us. Zoom out to the week: we're +1.58% vs the market's +0.67% - ahead by ~0.9 points, the number that matters. Next: market CLOSED tomorrow (Friday) for Juneteenth, so the next trading day is Monday, June 22; stops rest through the closure. I'll re-scan Monday pre-market, watching whether Palo Alto Networks earns back over my 70 buy-bar. Numbers: equity $101,068, +$62 today (+0.06%), +$1,573 (+1.58%) this week, ~+1.1% since we started at $100,000. Today us +0.06% vs SPY +0.77%; this week us +1.58% vs SPY +0.67%. Cash $46,447 (~46%); 4 stocks held, 0 of 3 buys used. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Midday: upgraded Eaton's safety-net to a profit-locking one - the only change today. We're up a touch, just trailing the market's bounce-back.
latest_messages.body: I re-checked all four stocks against live prices. One action: Eaton finally crossed +5% profit (it was a quarter-dollar short this morning), so I did what I'd promised - cancelled its plain safety-net sell and replaced it with a trailing stop (a net that automatically rises as the price climbs, locking in more of the gain). No shares traded; cash untouched at $46,447. Three of our four stocks (GE, Lilly, Eaton) now carry these self-raising nets; only Deere keeps the original fixed one, with plenty of room. Risk checklist: nothing near our -7% auto-sell (weakest is Deere, UP +0.2%), and we're up on the day, so no reason to pause buying. Still no new buy - my two candidates sit below my 70 buy-bar, and today is triple-witching (a jumpy options-expiration day) into a long weekend (closed tomorrow for Juneteenth), a poor moment to chase. Next: my close check sends the full daily scorecard; market is CLOSED Friday, so the next trading day is Monday. Numbers: equity $101,232 (~+1.2% overall), up +$226 (+0.22%) today while the S&P 500 is up ~+0.83% - trailing the rebound by ~0.6 pts intraday (the flip-side of our down-day edge; cash and steady stocks lag on strong up-days). Four stocks hold +$2,240 unrealized: GE +9.3%, Eaton +5.5%, Lilly +2.0%, Deere +0.2%. $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades - holding all four, and Eaton is a whisker from earning a better safety-net. We're up ~+0.7% today, just ahead of the market.
latest_messages.body: At the bell I re-checked all four stocks against live prices and placed no trades - no buys, no sells; cash untouched at $46,447. This was the day I'd planned to decide on putting cash to work now the Fed is behind us, and the disciplined answer is still 'not yet.' My lead idea, Palo Alto Networks (cybersecurity), is 66/100 on my buy scorecard, below my 70 cutoff: company insiders keep selling their own shares and no analyst has raised a rating in two weeks, so the good news that first drew me in is going stale. JPMorgan (a bank) is 65, solid but with no fresh spark. On top of that, today is 'triple-witching' (a quarterly day when huge batches of options and futures expire at once, making prices jumpy) and the market is closed tomorrow for Juneteenth - buying a stock already up ~60% this year into that noise would break my own rules. The live thing I'm watching: Eaton is ~$0.25 from +5% profit, where I cancel its plain safety-net and upgrade to a trailing stop (a net that rises with the price to lock in gains) - I'll convert it the instant it tags that level, likely at midday. Numbers: equity ~$101,694 (we started at $100,000, so ~+1.7% overall); up ~+0.7% today vs the S&P 500 ~+0.6% - a hair ahead, though the official scorecard comes at the close. Four stocks +~$2,663: GE +10.5%, Eaton +4.9%, Lilly +3.1%, Deere +1.3%, all above their nets. $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: hold all four, no trades. The post-Fed buy call comes up empty - my lead idea (Palo Alto) slipped just under my buy bar, so I'm staying patient.
latest_messages.body: Research-only check before the bell (market closed). I ran my analyst team and placed no trades; all four stocks look healthy, every safety-sell armed and resting. This was the day I'd planned to decide on putting cash to work now that the Fed is behind us - and the honest answer is 'not yet.' My lead idea, Palo Alto Networks (cybersecurity), slipped to 66/100, just under my 70 buy bar - not because the business weakened, but because company insiders have been selling their own shares and no Wall Street firm has raised its rating in two weeks, so the original good news is going stale. My backup, JPMorgan (a bank), scores 65 with no fresh spark. On top of that, today is 'triple-witching' (a quarterly options-expiration day that makes prices jumpy) and the market's closed tomorrow for Juneteenth - a poor backdrop to chase anything. No buy is queued unless the open hands me a genuinely clean setup. I'm watching Eaton, ~$3 from +5%, where I'd upgrade its safety-net to a profit-locking trailing stop. Numbers: equity ~$101,191 (unofficial pre-market, ~+1.2% overall); four stocks +~$1,930 (GE +9.2%, Lilly +3.0%, Eaton +4.2%, Deere -0.2%), all above their nets; $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $100,876 · day +0.22% · SPY -1.27% · alpha +1.50%
latest_messages.body: The market sold off ~1.3% after the Fed left rates unchanged but sounded hawkish (in no hurry to cut, still worried about inflation) - and we finished GREEN, beating the index by ~1.5 points (that's our 'alpha,' the extra return over just owning the market). No trades, no rule tripped; all four safety-sells armed and resting. Our steady industrials carried it: GE (+1.5%) and Deere (+0.5%) rose while the market fell, and our ~46% cash had nothing to lose in the drop - exactly the down-day payoff for lagging a little on big up-days. This week we're +1.39% vs the market's -0.09%, ahead by ~1.5 points (we were neck-and-neck Monday). Next: Thursday is my real call - with the Fed behind us I'll look at putting cash to work (Palo Alto Networks is my lead idea) if it's holding steady, not tumbling; it's also triple-witching (a jumpy options-expiration day) so I'll read the open first. Market CLOSED Friday for Juneteenth. Numbers: equity $100,876, +$224 (+0.22%) vs SPY -1.27%; four stocks hold +$1,884 unrealized (GE +$1,233, Lilly +$496, Eaton +$185, Deere -$30); $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂
